state_transitions.trigger: start() called — initiates GraphQL + RabbitMQ connections
GraphQL endpoint accessible and RabbitMQ (if configured) connects
GraphQL or RabbitMQ health check fails 3 consecutive times
Both GraphQL and RabbitMQ (if subscribed) health checks pass

params.name: cmms_graphql_url
params.description: CMMS GraphQL endpoint (e.g., http://localhost:3000/graphql). Primary data source for maintenance entities: assets, work orders, work requests, kits, inventory locations, investigations.

params.name: cmms_rest_url
params.description: CMMS REST API base URL (e.g., http://localhost:3000/api). Secondary source for reference data: vendors, items, users.

params.name: rabbitmq_url
params.description: RabbitMQ AMQP URL (e.g., amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672). Optional — required only if CMMS will subscribe to ERPI master data topics (item, vendor, inventory).

params.name: jwt_token
params.description: JWT bearer token for authenticated GraphQL/REST requests. Not required if CMMS has unauthenticated endpoints.

params.name: rabbitmq_consumer_group
params.description: Consumer group ID for RabbitMQ queue naming. Must be unique to Forge to avoid stealing messages from other consumers.

params.name: poll_interval_seconds
params.description: GraphQL polling interval for work orders, assets, maintenance requests (10-3600 seconds).

data_sources.name: CMMS GraphQL
data_sources.description: Primary source: GraphQL polling of CMMS maintenance entities. Polls at configurable interval (default 60s).

data_sources.name: CMMS REST API
data_sources.description: Secondary source: REST API for historical data and reference lookups.

data_sources.name: ERPI RabbitMQ
data_sources.description: Tertiary source: RabbitMQ subscription to ERPI master data topics for inventory enrichment.

cross_module_flows.data_flow: ERPI → CMMS (item, vendor, purchase order master data)
cross_module_flows.purpose: Enrich maintenance cost tracking with master item data and vendor information

cross_module_flows.data_flow: CMMS → MES (maintenance windows, asset state, approval status)
cross_module_flows.purpose: Production scheduling must account for scheduled maintenance windows that block equipment
Inventory audit reconciliation coordinates with warehouse operations

forge_fields: status (Scheduled → SCHEDULED, Active → IN_PROGRESS, Completed → COMPLETE)
status (Pending → DRAFT, Approved → SCHEDULED, Rejected → CANCELLED)

approval_workflows.description: Maintenance requests must be approved by maintenance supervisor, then operations supervisor before scheduling

audit_trail: All work order status transitions logged
Maintenance tech assignments tracked with timestamps
Approval decisions captured with user ID and timestamp
Cost calculations auditable via actualDuration and costCalculation fields

periodic_maintenance.description: WorkOrders and WorkRequests can have cronSchedule (e.g., '0 9 * * 0' = every Monday 9am) for preventive maintenance.
periodic_maintenance.example: Asset pump requires inspection every 30 days — cronSchedule generates recurring WorkOrders automatically

maintenance_kits.description: WorkOrders reference Kits, which contain pre-assembled sets of items for specific maintenance tasks.
maintenance_kits.example: Oil change kit includes filter, gasket, drain pan — allocated to work order for cost tracking

approval_governance.description: WorkRequests require dual approval (maintenance supervisor + operations supervisor) before scheduling to prevent conflicts with production.
approval_governance.example: Operator requests emergency bearing replacement; maintenance supervisor approves urgency; operations supervisor schedules maintenance window

production_impact.description: Scheduled maintenance windows (from WorkOrder.scheduledStart/End) must be communicated to MES for production scheduling constraints.
production_impact.example: Distillery scheduled for maintenance 2024-04-15 08:00-12:00 UTC blocks ProductionOrders with those timestamps

phase_1_limitations.no_graphql_mutations: Phase 1 reads CMMS state only; cannot create/update work orders, assets, or requests via Forge
phase_1_limitations.no_rabbitmq_publishing: Phase 1 subscribes to ERPI topics only; cannot publish CMMS events to RabbitMQ
phase_1_limitations.graphql_polling: Phase 2 will add live polling; Phase 1 uses inject_records() for testing

related_adapters.relationship: ERPI provides master item and vendor data via RabbitMQ topics; CMMS enriches maintenance cost tracking with this data
related_adapters.data_flow: ERPI item/vendor topics → CMMS RabbitMQ subscriptions

related_adapters.relationship: MES must account for CMMS maintenance windows when scheduling production orders
related_adapters.data_flow: CMMS scheduled maintenance windows → MES production scheduling constraints

related_adapters.relationship: WMS coordinates with CMMS on inventory investigations and physical counts
related_adapters.data_flow: CMMS inventory investigations ↔ WMS barrel inventory reconciliation
